what: From records to a fentanyl flag
title: Age-standardized fentanyl death rates by sex
text: Start handful illustrative record-level rows. Real MCOD data carry restatus (residency), ucod (underlying cause), f_records_all (space-joined multiple-cause T-code string) flag helpers key . subset_residents() keeps restatus %% 1:3 drops column. non-resident row (restatus == 4) removed. flag_drug_deaths() adds drug_death ISW7 (Injury Surveillance Workgroup) rule – drug ucod T-code. cardiac death (I250) drug death. flag_opioid_deaths() adds opioid_death. flag_opioid_types() splits opioid type flags; other_synth_present T40.4 fentanyl proxy. Note T40.0-row (opium, T40.0) opioid death synthetic-opioid death. fentanyl-proxy deaths rows other_synth_present == 1:
code: records <- data.frame(     year          = 2020L,     restatus      = c(1L, 1L, 4L, 2L, 3L),     ucod          = c("X42", "X44", "X42", "I250", "X42"),     f_records_all = c("T404 T519", "T404", "T404", "I250", "T400"),     age           = c(35L, 40L, 55L, 60L, 25L),     sex           = c("male", "female", "male", "male", "female"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) records #>   year restatus ucod f_records_all age    sex #> 1 2020        1  X42     T404 T519  35   male #> 2 2020        1  X44          T404  40 female #> 3 2020        4  X42          T404  55   male #> 4 2020        2 I250          I250  60   male #> 5 2020        3  X42          T400  25 female resid <- subset_residents(records) resid #>   year ucod f_records_all age    sex #> 1 2020  X42     T404 T519  35   male #> 2 2020  X44          T404  40 female #> 3 2020 I250          I250  60   male #> 4 2020  X42          T400  25 female resid <- flag_drug_deaths(resid, year = 2020) resid[, c("ucod", "f_records_all", "drug_death")] #>   ucod f_records_all drug_death #> 1  X42     T404 T519          1 #> 2  X44          T404          1 #> 3 I250          I250          0 #> 4  X42          T400          1 resid <- resid |>     flag_opioid_deaths(year = 2020) |>     flag_opioid_types(year = 2020) resid[, c("f_records_all", "opioid_death", "other_synth_present")] #>   f_records_all opioid_death other_synth_present #> 1     T404 T519            1                   1 #> 2          T404            1                   1 #> 3          I250            0                   0 #> 4          T400            1                   0 resid[resid$other_synth_present == 1, c("ucod", "f_records_all", "age", "sex")] #>   ucod f_records_all age    sex #> 1  X42     T404 T519  35   male #> 2  X44          T404  40 female

title: Age-standardized fentanyl death rates by sex
text: practice flag every record way, aggregate fentanyl deaths year x age x sex counts computing rates. build aggregated frame directly . counts illustrative – deterministic Poisson draw tuned rise steeply time (real synthetic-opioid surge) run higher males. age 5-year bin start (0, 5, …, 85); sex "male"/"female". add_pop_counts(race_scheme = "bridged") joins SEER bridged-race denominator adds pop. Race by_var, pop -race total year x age x sex cell; bridged requires year by_vars. state_fips/county_fips, national denominator used automatically. add_std_pop() adds US 2000 standard population (pop_std) unit weights (unit_w, summing 1 across 18 five-year age groups). s204 narcan’s code standard population 18 five-year age bins (default std_cat); see ?add_std_pop single-year alternatives, must match age binning. calc_asrate_var() adds age-specific rate (fentanyl_rate, per 100,000) Poisson variance (fentanyl_var) age-sex-year cell – rate standardization. 2020 male age-specific rates, peak early adulthood (~age 30) decline thereafter: calc_stdrate_var() collapses age bins one age-standardized rate per year x sex, reweighting stratum US 2000 age structure. Pass grouping columns (year, sex) explicitly – added automatically.

title: Age-standardized fentanyl death rates by sex
text: illustrative data male rate runs roughly twice female rate every year, rise 35-40 fold 1999 2020 – shape real synthetic-opioid epidemic, exact levels. 95% confidence interval follows returned variance fentanyl_rate +/- 1.96 * sqrt(fentanyl_var): sexes standardized US 2000 age structure, male-female gap reflects differences death rates, age composition – point age standardization. counts synthetic illustrative . real analysis flags restricted public MCOD records exactly shown (see “Classifying overdose deaths” vignette), aggregates , runs rate pipeline.
code: std <- as.data.frame(std) std$lower <- std$fentanyl_rate - 1.96 * sqrt(std$fentanyl_var) std$upper <- std$fentanyl_rate + 1.96 * sqrt(std$fentanyl_var) std[, c("year", "sex", "fentanyl_rate", "lower", "upper")] #>   year    sex fentanyl_rate      lower      upper #> 1 1999 female     0.3249014  0.2951610  0.3546417 #> 2 1999   male     0.6528509  0.6104495  0.6952524 #> 3 2010 female     1.8209196  1.7517748  1.8900644 #> 4 2010   male     3.6311987  3.5333292  3.7290682 #> 5 2020 female    12.3640330 12.1862739 12.5417921 #> 6 2020   male    22.9116102 22.6715346 23.1516859

what: Run the flag pipeline, one step at a time
title: Classifying overdose deaths (ISW7)
text: Three flaggers run sequence. one adds columns never drops row, can watch classification build step step. Pass year select coding era. real use, f_records_all hand-built – read raw fixed-width MCOD file import_mcod_fwf() collapse multiple-cause fields unite_records(), newcomer real data start. Step 1. flag_drug_deaths() adds drug_death. two non-drug rows (I250, C509) score 0; every drug-poisoning row scores 1, including psychostimulant row (e.g. methamphetamine) opioid. Step 2. flag_opioid_deaths() adds opioid_death. lights drug death also carries opioid T-code, X40 / T436 stays 0. Step 3. flag_opioid_types() adds six subtype indicators plus num_opioids (multi_opioids). polydrug row (T401 T404) now reads two distinct opioids.
code: step1 <- flag_drug_deaths(resident_deaths, year = 2019L) step1[, c("ucod", "f_records_all", "drug_death")] #>   ucod f_records_all drug_death #> 1  X42          T400          1 #> 2  X42     T401 T404          1 #> 3  X44          T402          1 #> 4  X42          T403          1 #> 5  X42          T404          1 #> 6  Y12          T406          1 #> 7  X40          T436          1 #> 8 I250          I250          0 #> 9 C509          C509          0 step2 <- flag_opioid_deaths(step1, year = 2019L) step2[, c("ucod", "f_records_all", "drug_death", "opioid_death")] #>   ucod f_records_all drug_death opioid_death #> 1  X42          T400          1            1 #> 2  X42     T401 T404          1            1 #> 3  X44          T402          1            1 #> 4  X42          T403          1            1 #> 5  X42          T404          1            1 #> 6  Y12          T406          1            1 #> 7  X40          T436          1            0 #> 8 I250          I250 0           1 #> 2     T401 T404            1              1                   1           2 #> 3          T402            1              0                   0           1 #> 4          T403 title: Classifying overdose deaths (ISW7)
text: definitions nest. drug death requires drug-poisoning underlying cause (X40-44, X60-64, X85, Y10-14) drug T-code (T36.0-T50.9). opioid death strict subset drug deaths also carry opioid T-code (T40.0-T40.4 T40.6). poisoning non-opioid drug drug death opioid death. Row X40 / T436 (psychostimulant, e.g. methamphetamine) exactly edge case – drug_death 1, opioid_death 0.
code: classified[classified$ucod == "X40",            c("ucod", "f_records_all", "drug_death", "opioid_death")] #>   ucod f_records_all drug_death opioid_death #> 7  X40          T436          1            0

title: Classifying overdose deaths (ISW7)
text: Among opioid deaths, flag_opioid_types() breaks six specific T40 subtypes – opium_present (T40.0), heroin_present (T40.1), other_natural_present (T40.2), methadone_present (T40.3), other_synth_present (T40.4, fentanyl proxy), other_op_present (T40.6). unspecified_op_present residual opioid death none six matched, num_opioids counts many distinct subtypes appear record. polydrug row (T401 T404) shows num_opioids = 2.
code: subtype_cols <- c(     "opium_present", "heroin_present", "other_natural_present",     "methadone_present", "other_synth_present", "other_op_present",     "unspecified_op_present", "num_opioids" ) classified[classified$opioid_death == 1, c("f_records_all", subtype_cols)] #>   f_records_all opium_present heroin_present other_natural_present #> 1          T400             1              0                     0 #> 2     T401 T404             0              1                     0 #> 3          T402             0              0                     1 #> 4          T403             0              0                     0 #> 5          T404             0              0                     0 #> 6          T406             0              0                     0 #>   methadone_present other_synth_present other_op_present unspecified_op_present #> 1                 0                   0                0                      0 #> 2                 0                   1                0                      0 #> 3                 0                   0                0                      0 #> 4                 1                   0                0                      0 #> 5                 0                   1                0                      0 #> 6                 0                   0                1                      0 #>   num_opioids #> 1           1 #> 2           2 #> 3           1 #> 4           1 #> 5           1 #> 6           1

what: state_abbrev_to_fips() – one column at a time
title: Harmonizing geography with FIPS
text: simplest helper maps postal abbreviation zero-padded 2-digit state FIPS. Pair input output see mapping: "CA" becomes "06", "6" – zero-padding lets result feed FIPS-keyed join. Unrecognized abbreviations foreign/unknown code "ZZ" return NA (warning) rather passing unconverted.
code: abbrevs <- c("CA", "NY", "TX", "PA") data.frame(abbrev = abbrevs, state_fips = state_abbrev_to_fips(abbrevs)) #>   abbrev state_fips #> 1     CA         06 #> 2     NY         36 #> 3     TX         48 #> 4     PA         42

title: Harmonizing geography with FIPS
text: Feed add_county_fips() raw county column character (countyrs county residence, countyoc occurrence). numeric column refused, numeric input already dropped leading zero (01001 reads 1001, whose first two digits parse different state). Pass year – scalar year column – coding scheme chosen deterministically. Start small synthetic frame straddles 2002/2003 boundary. 1999-2002 rows carry NCHS numeric state codes; 2003-2004 rows carry postal abbreviations, exactly public files : Now harmonize. call adds two columns – st_fips (2-digit state) county_fips (5-digit state + county) – decoding row era’s scheme: Read /together. raw countyrs string "06031" (1999) maps st_fips "08" county_fips "08031" – NCHS "06" Colorado, California. 2003, California enters instead abbreviation "CA", giving "06075". single numeric code like "06..." therefore ambiguous ; add_county_fips() resolves row row year, one frame spanning eras comes consistent 5-digit FIPS grain. Codes place – "ZZ" foreign residence, ambiguous NCHS code "62" – yield NA rather spurious string.
code: deaths <- tibble::tibble(     id       = 1:5,     year     = c(1999L, 2001L, 2002L, 2003L, 2004L),     countyrs = c("06031", "36061", "48059", "CA075", "TX201") ) deaths #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>      id  year countyrs #>   <int> <int> <chr>    #> 1     1  1999 06031    #> 2     2  2001 36061    #> 3     3  2002 48059    #> 4     4  2003 CA075    #> 5     5  2004 TX201 harmonized <- add_county_fips(deaths, countyrs) harmonized[c("id", "year", "countyrs", "st_fips", "county_fips")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>      id  year countyrs st_fips county_fips #>   <int> <int> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>       #> 1     1  1999 06031    08      08031       #> 2     2  2001 36061    39      39061       #> 3     3  2002 48059    53      53059       #> 4     4  2003 CA075    06      06075       #> 5     5  2004 TX201    48      48201

title: Population denominators: bridged, single-race, and legacy
text: race_scheme = "legacy" joins frozen pop_est reproduces historical behavior byte--byte: unmatched keys warn leave pop = NA. Use reproduce published bridged-race rates. 2000-2020 legacy denominator single-race-alone (runs low bridged-race deaths), add_pop_counts() nudges per session span toward "bridged" "single".
code: legacy <- data.frame(year = 1999L, age = 25L, sex = "male", race = "white") add_pop_counts(legacy)                    # default scheme; adds `pop` #>   year age  sex  race     pop #> 1 1999  25 male white 7289220

title: Population denominators: bridged, single-race, and legacy
text: race_scheme = "single" joins single-race denominators (2000-2024) deaths coded 101-106 (white_only/black_only/american_indian_only/asian_only/ nhopi_only/multiracial). strict: --domain age/sex/race unmatched key hard-error, denominator never silently NA. full asian_only age-standardized rate, one step time. add_pop_counts() adds matched pop column: add_std_pop() adds US 2000 standard population (pop_std) unit weights (unit_w, summing 1 across age groups). s204 narcan’s code standard population 18 five-year age bins (default std_cat); see ?add_std_pop single-year alternatives, must match age binning: calc_asrate_var() adds age-specific rate per 100,000 (drug_rate) Poisson variance (drug_var) – one rate per age/sex cell: calc_stdrate_var() collapses age bins one age-standardized rate per group (grouping automatic – pass every dimension keep):
code: deaths <- expand.grid(     year = 2024L, age = seq(0, 85, 5), sex = c("male", "female"),     race = "asian_only", stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) deaths$deaths <- rep(c(1, 2, 5, 12, 30, 55), length.out = nrow(deaths)) head(deaths) #>   year age  sex       race deaths #> 1 2024   0 male asian_only      1 #> 2 2024   5 male asian_only      2 #> 3 2024  10 male asian_only      5 #> 4 2024  15 male asian_only     12 #> 5 2024  20 male asian_only     30 #> 6 2024  25 male asian_only     55 rated <- add_pop_counts(deaths, race_scheme = "single") head(rated) #>   year age  sex       race deaths    pop #> 1 2024   0 male asian_only      1 600728 #> 2 2024   5 male asian_only      2 678658 #> 3 2024  10 male asian_only      5 659666 #> 4 2024  15 male asian_only     12 666097 #> 5 2024  20 male asian_only     30 760220 #> 6 2024  25 male asian_only     55 826163 weighted <- add_std_pop(rated, std_cat = "s204", by_vars = "age") head(weighted[, c("age", "sex", "pop", "pop_std", "unit_w")]) #>   age  sex    pop  pop_std     unit_w #> 1   0 male 600728 18986520 0.06913399 #> 2   5 male 678658 19919840 0.07253241 #> 3  10 male 659666 20056779 0.07303103 #> 4  15 male 666097 19819518 0.07216712 #> 5  20 male 760220 18257225 0.06647847 #> 6  25 male 826163 17722067 0.06452985 asr <- calc_asrate_var(weighted, new_name = drug, death_col = deaths) head(asr[, c("age", "sex", "deaths", "pop", "drug_rate", "drug_var")]) #>   age  sex deaths    pop drug_rate   drug_var #> 1   0 male      1 600728 0.1664647 0.02771049 #> 2   5 male      2 678658 0.2946992 0.04342382 #> 3  10 male      5 659666 0.7579593 0.11490047 #> 4  15 male     12 666097 1.8015394 0.27046202 #> 5  20 male     30 760220 3.9462261 0.51909001 #> 6  25 male     55 826163 6.6572819 0.80580732 calc_stdrate_var(asr, drug_rate, drug_var, sex, race) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #> # Groups:   sex [2] #>   sex    race       drug_rate drug_var #>   <chr>  <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 female asian_only      2.48   0.0199 #> 2 male   asian_only      2.91   0.0274

title: Population denominators: bridged, single-race, and legacy
text: race_scheme = "bridged" joins SEER bridged denominators (1969-2024). strict era-ragged: SEER resolves AIAN/API Hispanic origin 1990 (pre-1990 white/black/), year MUST join key. Omitting year by_vars error – valid race set era-dependent: api row 1990 errors, SEER AIAN/API split yet:
code: api <- data.frame(year = 2019L, age = 40L, sex = "female", race = "api") add_pop_counts(api, race_scheme = "bridged",                by_vars = c("year", "age", "sex", "race")) #>   year age    sex race    pop #> 1 2019  40 female  api 874749 add_pop_counts(api, race_scheme = "bridged",                by_vars = c("age", "sex", "race")) #> Error: #> ! add_pop_counts(): `df` carries population-dimension column(s) `year` not in `by_vars`; under a strict race_scheme ("single"/"bridged") the denominator would be silently summed over them. Add them to `by_vars`, or drop them from `df` to aggregate that dimension. old_api <- data.frame(year = 1985L, age = 40L, sex = "female", race = "api") add_pop_counts(old_api, race_scheme = "bridged",                by_vars = c("year", "age", "sex", "race")) #> Error: #> ! add_pop_counts(): race value(s) 'api' are not denominable under race_scheme = "bridged" before 1990 (SEER pre-1990 race = white/black/other only; the AIAN/API split begins in 1990). Valid pre-1990: white, black, other (or "total"). Restrict to year >= 1990, or collapse to `other`.

title: Population denominators: bridged, single-race, and legacy
text: Legacy bridged share labels white/black//total, mis-set race_scheme always detected. single-race labels non-single scheme always can – passing default hard error, guard forgetting race_scheme = "single": frame already summed dimension uses reserved token – race = "total", sex = "", hispanic_origin = "". matching marginal synthesized demand (finest cells summed; marginal stored, double-count):
code: bad <- data.frame(year = 2024L, age = 25L, sex = "male", race = "asian_only") add_pop_counts(bad)                       # default is "legacy" -> error #> Error: #> ! add_pop_counts(): `race` holds single-race values (101-106 or *_only/multiracial) but race_scheme is not "single". Pass race_scheme = "single" to use single-race denominators. both_sex <- data.frame(year = 2024L, age = 65L, sex = "both", race = "asian_only") add_pop_counts(both_sex, race_scheme = "single")$pop        # male + female #> [1] 1042672

title: Trends in unspecified drug overdose deaths
text: frame synthetic illustrative. row one death, coded ICD-10 convention – ucod underlying-cause code f_records_all space-joined multiple-cause T-code string. Opioid deaths carry poisoning ucod (X42, accidental poisoning narcotics) plus one T40.x opioid code: heroin (T40.1), synthetic opioids fentanyl (T40.4), “unspecified” residual (T40.6). Two cocaine deaths per year (T40.5) included opioid filter something remove. Counts chosen T40.6 share falls deaths get specific opioid type time.
code: recipe <- tibble::tribble(     ~year, ~ucod, ~f_records_all, ~n,     1999L, "X42", "T401",         4L,   # heroin     1999L, "X42", "T404",         2L,   # synthetic (e.g. fentanyl)     1999L, "X42", "T406",         4L,   # other/unspecified opioid     1999L, "X42", "T405",         2L,   # cocaine (non-opioid drug death)     2005L, "X42", "T401",         5L,     2005L, "X42", "T404",         3L,     2005L, "X42", "T406",         4L,     2005L, "X42", "T405",         2L,     2012L, "X42", "T401",         6L,     2012L, "X42", "T404",         6L,     2012L, "X42", "T406",         3L,     2012L, "X42", "T405",         2L,     2019L, "X42", "T401",         8L,     2019L, "X42", "T404",         9L,     2019L, "X42", "T406",         3L,     2019L, "X42", "T405",         2L ) deaths <- recipe[rep(seq_len(nrow(recipe)), recipe$n),                  c("year", "ucod", "f_records_all")] rownames(deaths) <- NULL nrow(deaths) #> [1] 65

what: Walk the flags, one step at a time
title: Trends in unspecified drug overdose deaths
text: flag keys ucod plus f_records_all, ICD era read year, run pipeline one year time. 2012 slice step. flag_drug_deaths() adds drug_death – ICD-10 1 qualifying poisoning ucod pairs drug T-code. four 2012 record types qualify. flag_opioid_deaths() adds opioid_death, additionally requires T40.0-.4 T40.6 opioid code. three opioid rows stay 1; cocaine death (T40.5) drops 0. flag_opioid_types() adds six specific-type columns, ICD-9-era residual unspecified_op_present, num_opioids. T40.6 deaths land other_op_present; unspecified_op_present stays 0 (see closing note).
code: d12 <- deaths[deaths$year == 2012, ] s1 <- flag_drug_deaths(d12, year = 2012) head(s1) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>    year ucod  f_records_all drug_death #>   <int> <chr> <chr>              <dbl> #> 1  2012 X42   T401                   1 #> 2  2012 X42   T401                   1 #> 3  2012 X42   T401                   1 #> 4  2012 X42   T401                   1 #> 5  2012 X42   T401                   1 #> 6  2012 X42   T401                   1 s2 <- flag_opioid_deaths(s1, year = 2012) unique(subset(s2, f_records_all %in% c("T401", "T406", "T405")))  # heroin, other/unspecified, cocaine #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>    year ucod  f_records_all drug_death opioid_death #>   <int> <chr> <chr>              <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1  2012 X42   T401                   1            1 #> 2  2012 X42   T406                   1            1 #> 3  2012 X42   T405                   1            0 s3 <- flag_opioid_types(s2, year = 2012) type_cols <- c("ucod", "f_records_all", "opioid_death", "heroin_present",                "other_synth_present", "other_op_present",                "unspecified_op_present", "num_opioids") as.data.frame(unique(s3[s3$opioid_death == 1, type_cols])) #>   ucod f_records_all opioid_death heroin_present other_synth_present #> 1  X42          T401            1              1                   0 #> 2  X42          T404            1              0                   1 #> 3  X42          T406            1              0                   0 #>   other_op_present unspecified_op_present num_opioids #> 1                0                      0           1 #> 2                0                      0           1 #> 3                1                      0           1

title: Trends in unspecified drug overdose deaths
text: Apply three steps every year, keep opioid deaths, take mean other_op_present per year – mean T40.6 share. T40.6 share falls 0.40 1999 0.15 2019 – built illustrative counts, identical computation real MCOD records recovers whatever trend data actually hold.
code: flagged <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(split(deaths, deaths$year), function(d) {     y <- d$year[1]     d |>         flag_drug_deaths(year = y) |>         flag_opioid_deaths(year = y) |>         flag_opioid_types(year = y) }))  unspec <- flagged |>     dplyr::filter(opioid_death == 1) |>     dplyr::group_by(year) |>     dplyr::summarize(         opioid_deaths = dplyr::n(),         t406_unspecified = sum(other_op_present),         t406_share = round(mean(other_op_present), 3),         .groups = "drop"     ) unspec #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>    year opioid_deaths t406_unspecified t406_share #>   <int>         <int>            <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1  1999            10                4      0.4   #> 2  2005            12                4      0.333 #> 3  2012            15                3      0.2   #> 4  2019            20                3      0.15

title: Trends in unspecified drug overdose deaths
text: T40.6 standard ICD-10 proxy unspecified opioid, label “unspecified narcotics”, T40.6 death one specific opioid distinguished, strictly one missing. opioid type-unspecified share, broader problem drug deaths specific drug coded , spans non-opioid drugs . narcan also carries unspecified_op_present, ICD-9-era residual keyed generic opiate code 965.0; 0 construction years >= 1999 ICD-10 folds unspecified opioids T40.6, exactly modern measure reads other_op_present instead. analysts redistribute T40.6 deaths across known types computing drug-specific rates; narcan gives raw flags can measure unspecified share first decide adjust .

text: NCHS mortality files code state way every era, scheme must identified can translated: NCHS numeric codes overlap FIPS numeric codes mean *different* states (NCHS Colorado `"06"` FIPS California), bare 2-digit numeric code ambiguous . resolved data year: pass `year` (scalar, leave `NULL` read `year` column `df`) scheme chosen deterministically. year available function fall back guessing observed codes, **warns loudly** whenever codes ambiguous. `"fips"` never auto-detected numeric codes year >= 2003 (raw files use abbreviations ); pass `scheme = "fips"` explicitly data already converted. Use reproduce published bridged-race rates. race_scheme = "single" joins single-race denominators (pop_singlerace_full, 2000-2024; frozen pop_singlerace 2020-2024 slice used unchanged pre-2020 year requested) deaths coded remap_race()/categorize_race() codes 101-106. race_scheme = "bridged" joins SEER-uniform bridged-race denominators (pop_bridged, 1969-2024) deaths coded bridged categories. strict: guarantee silent NA denominator, --domain age/sex/race values hard-error rather passing . "total" (race), "" (sex), "" (Hispanic origin) aggregate tokens synthesized demand. "bridged" scheme era-ragged: SEER resolves AIAN/API Hispanic origin 1990 (pre-1990 white/black/), REQUIRES year by_vars validates race set per row row's era. Asian/Pacific-Islander subgroups (chinese/japanese/hawaiian/ filipino) separate bridged denominator; collapse api (numerator denominator together) joining.

what: New features – single-race backfill to 2000 + SEER-uniform bridged denominators
title: narcan 0.5.1
text: Single-race denominators extended back 2000. pop_singlerace_full (national, bundled) now spans 2000-2024, backfilling 0.5.0 tables (covered 2020-2024). State county backfills ship Release assets. 0.5.0 pop_singlerace/pop_singlerace_state (2020-2024) frozen byte--byte; backfill additive 2020-2024 slice identical frozen tables. SEER-uniform bridged-race denominators, 1969-2024. New pop_bridged (national, bundled) plus state/county Release assets give single, internally consistent bridged-race series SEER U.S. Population Data. Unlike legacy pop_est (pieced-together vintages, stops 2020), one series covers 1969-2024 national/state/county. era-ragged: SEER resolves AIAN/API Hispanic origin 1990 (pre-1990 white/black/), bridged scheme requires year validates race set per row’s era. add_pop_counts() gains race_scheme = "bridged" alongside "legacy" (default) "single". strict schemes guarantee silent NA denominator; request reaches year outside scheme’s coverage hard error, silent empty result. Single-race requests routed pre-2020 year automatically use backfill; 2020-2024-requests stay dependency-free bundled tables. get_pop_state()/get_pop_county() gain bridged scheme honor backfill (pass pre-2020 years reach 2000-2024); get_pop_state() gains parquet= argument. download_pop_data(scheme = "bridged") fetches bridged parquets; pop_sources() lists every single-race bridged dataset. New vignettes: classifying-overdose-deaths (ISW7 drug/opioid flagging), age-standardized-rates (end--end age-standardized synthetic-opioid rate sex, US residents), population-denominators (choosing among three schemes; supersedes single-race-rates), geography-fips (FIPS harmonization, public geography 1999-2004), unspecified-drug-deaths (specificity problem time).

ICD-9 era now runs idempotent clean_icd9_data() internally collapsing record columns. Previously, calling documented pipeline (unite_records() -> flag_drug_deaths()/flag_opioid_deaths()/ flag_opioid_types()) raw ICD-9 frame silently returned -zero drug/opioid flags (mis-formatted E-codes nature--injury codes missed ICD-9 regex) errored rniflag_-named nature--injury columns (1991-1995 files). Blast radius: pre-1999 analyses already call clean_icd9_data() first – flag counts change wrong correct. Already-cleaned pipelines unchanged (cleaner idempotent). add_coded_occupation() returns type-stable, zero-padded codes. occ_coded/ind_coded now zero-padded character eras. Previously 1982-1999 Census (3-digit) scheme returned numeric codes leading zeros dropped (e.g. 7 occupation "007"), broke 3-digit crosswalks made bind_rows() pre-2000 result 2020+ NIOSH (character) result error type clash.
